groups.context_description: Term names divine indignation as purposive instrument of judgment — God's inner wrath deployed purposively against wickedness and idolatry
Term names the prophet's inner state as divinely imparted indignation — God's own indignation placed within the person called to speak for him
Term names rage as inner fury producing visible physical transformation and extreme action — the Aramaic term for anger at its most intense somatic expression
Term names divine wrath as consuming inner fire — the heat of God's anger poured out in judgment, spending itself completely
Term names anger as the inner state that transforms countenance and drives extreme action — human fury at its most visible and disruptive
Term names the inner heat of habitual anger — the fury characteristic as a settled disposition that requires management and produces destruction when uncontrolled
Term names the inner warming process of developing intensity — anger or passionate feeling as a thermal process building from within through meditation
Term names the kindling of inner anger as the first act of moral rebellion — Cain's anger at divine rejection as the origin-point of the anger characteristic in Scripture
Term names divine anger as the inner response to Israel's repeated covenant violation — the burning of God's wrath in the moment of particular transgression
Term names the inner injunction against anger as spiritual counsel — do not let anger kindle; cease from it; it leads only to harm
Term names the burning intensity of divine wrath at its maximum — fierce anger as the pinnacle of God's response to covenant violation, carrying cosmic consequences
Term names human burning anger as the inner response to perceived injustice or affront — the fierce heat of personal wrath that drives consequential action
Term names divine relational anger — God's inner anger toward specific persons in response to covenant violation, expressed personally and resolved personally
Term names quick-temperedness as a disqualifying character trait — habitual anger as evidence of inner instability incompatible with leadership
Term names the anger that hardens into danger without resolution — the sunset-bounded inner state that must be addressed before becoming settled resentment
Term names the excellent spirit as the seat of extraordinary inner character — wisdom, understanding, and moral quality concentrated in the inner spirit as divine endowment
Term names the hardened spirit as the inner seat of pride — the spirit that stiffens against God, producing arrogant conduct and consequent judgment
Term names the anxious spirit as the inner reception of divine vision — the spirit overwhelmed and troubled by apocalyptic revelation
Term names inner fury driving political and power conflicts — fierce anger in an authority figure expressed through hostile action
Term names the provoking of divine vexation — Israel's conduct as the trigger of God's inner anger, creating a recurring covenantal pattern of provocation and response
Term names the inner vexation of relational cruelty — the distress produced in a person's inner life by another's deliberately provocative conduct
Term names the inner lodging of vexation as a marker of folly — anger that settles and remains as a characteristic of the unwise person
Term names the vexation of inner grief and provocation — the broad register of inner distress from existential burden, rivalry, and sorrow expressed before God in prayer
Term names the anger that lodges in the fool's heart — inner vexation as the resident condition of one who lacks inner governance
Term names the conduct that provokes divine inner anger — Israel's actions causing God's vexation, with covenantal consequences
Term names fierce inner anger co-existing with grief and loyalty — the intensity qualifier that characterises anger at its most burning while accompanying care
Term names the trembling restraint of anger — being agitated without sin, holding inner fury still before God in the privacy of the heart
Term names the somatic terror produced by divine power — the body overwhelmed by the experience of God's glory and judgment
Term names grief-disturbance — the agitation of inner pain expressing itself as trembling and mourning in response to devastating loss
Term names the provocation of divine agitation — conduct that disturbs God's inner relational rest, stirring him to respond
Term names the sharp inner conflict of disagreement — the cutting edge of contention between persons of conviction
Term names the deliberate inner-stimulation toward love and good works — the intentional provoking of others' inner life toward what is good
Term names blasphemous contempt as the inner disposition of rebellion — the speech-act of reviling that reveals the heart's rejection of what is holy
Term names disgrace as an inner condition in extremity — the shame-experience accompanying crisis that overwhelms all capacity for response
Term names the trembling heart as divinely imposed inner instability — inner anxiety and restlessness given by God as a consequence of covenantal displacement
Term names the prophetically embodied inner state of trembling anxiety — the prophet enacting the inner condition of those under judgment as a sign
Term names contempt as the inner disposition of treating what is sacred as beneath regard — the spurning of God, his word, and his servants
Term names contempt directed at reproof — the inner rejection of correction that closes the person to growth and repentance
Term names the inner speech of contempt — what the wicked person says in his heart as the location of his dismissal of God
Term names the inner bearing of grudge — sustained anger retained in the heart against another, named as the interior counterpart to the outer act of vengeance
Term names the duration of divine anger — how long God holds wrath and whether it resolves into mercy, with the answer consistently favouring mercy
Term names the fall of the face as the first bodily expression of inner anger — countenance as the mirror of the inner emotional state
Term names verbal aggression as the outer expression of inner hostility — railing and abuse as manifestations of the contemptuous inner character
Term names divine anger as the inner response to persistent covenant violation — God's wrath kindled by Israel's rebellion against his ways
Term names human anger as the inner response to subordinate failure or betrayal — the superior's wrath provoked by those under his authority
Term names divine anger as momentary against the backdrop of everlasting love — the transient quality of God's wrath contrasted with the permanence of his compassion
Term names the communal consequences of anger — how one person's anger destabilises the whole community around them
Term names the act of provoking divine inner anger — the causal triggering of God's wrath through covenant violation, with historical consequence
Term names inner fury triggered by perceived betrayal — the rage that erupts when deception is discovered, driving violent disproportionate response
Term names the inner capacity for endurance under increasing pressure — the ability of the inner person to sustain more difficult circumstances without collapsing
Term names competition as an exposure of inner character — striving for display reveals the poverty of the inner life compared with the practice of justice
Term names divine wrath as the revealed inner response to human unrighteousness and ungodliness — anger as the consistent inner orientation of God toward sin
Term names human anger as requiring governance — the inner state that must be put away, not nursed, as incompatible with righteous living
Term names righteous anger co-existing with grief — the simultaneous presence of anger and sorrow as the inner response to hardness of heart
Term names morally accountable anger — the inner state of anger that exists under divine command and requires governance before it becomes sin
Term names anger as an inner action-driver — the fury that compels the person to respond, act, or enforce consequences
Term names anger as cosmic inner rebellion against God — the hostility of nations and powers in ultimate opposition to divine rule
Term names righteous inner spirit-provocation — the spirit of the person stirred to righteous indignation by idolatry and the honour of God
Term names love's non-irritability — the inner disposition of love that displaces reactive anger and refuses to be provoked
Term names anger as a provoked inner response to injustice or disobedience — the arousal of wrath in another as consequence of conduct
Term names divine anger as the response to idolatry — the kindling of God's wrath at Israel's most direct covenant violation
Term names divine anger as tempered by steadfast love — the characterisation of God whose anger is slow and bounded by covenant faithfulness
Term names human anger as the inner response to relational conflict — the kindling of personal wrath in the face of provocation, rejection, or perceived injustice
Term names the mastery of anger as the measure of inner greatness — governing anger as exceeding military conquest in demonstration of inner strength
Term names divine anger as the experience of hiddenness — the face turned away as the form of wrath, producing inner desolation
Term names the inner heat of anger as a smoldering thermal process — the self-sustaining build of inner fury toward eruption, using the oven-and-fire metaphor
Act of embittering — causing bitterness in another through harsh treatment, or receiving revelation that brings both sweetness and bitter weight of judgment
Inner quality of bitterness characterising destructive inner dispositions — bitter jealousy as inner state of the heart
Inner quality of bitter weeping — grief of particular intensity saturated with self-recognition, shame, and weight of moral failure
Bitter rebellion — inner act of wilful embittered resistance against God's word after having heard it; hardening against divine revelation
Bitterness as destructive inner state — disposition of hostility, resentment, or hardness that corrupts the person and damages community
Bitterness as spreading inner root — deeply embedded condition that defiles and corrupts beyond the individual
State of being filled with bitterness — inner person pervaded by bitter anguish, here in context of divine affliction that allows no relief
Private interior quality of bitterness — heart's own bitter knowledge of itself, known to the inner person alone and incommunicable to any other
Bitterness as inner state caused by grief — specific pain of a parent whose child has brought shame; inner sorrow carrying quality of bitterness
Bitter toxic quality of suffering and judgment — poison/gall as inner experience of affliction brought before God, or as divine response to inner corruption
Inner condition of being saturated with bitterness — inner person overwhelmed with bitter suffering inflicted by divine judgment
God as Jealous — the divine inner passion that will not share worship with other gods
Zealotry as inner commitment — devoted passion for a cause, law, or calling
Hot or cold — the inner temperature of spiritual commitment and fervour
Fervency of spirit — the inner burning disposition of spiritual engagement
Rekindling the inner gift — stirring up spiritual vitality from within
God as jealous avenger — the inner passion of divine justice and exclusive claim
Earnest desire and eager zeal — the inner longing for what is good and spiritual
Jealousy and covetous desire — inner hostility and consuming want
Human jealousy and envy — resentment of another's blessing or standing
Zeal for God — passionate inner devotion expressed as jealous advocacy
Provoking God to jealousy — Israel's idolatry as inner spiritual betrayal
Envy as inner moral disposition — resentment of another's good and murderous rivalry
The eye as inner moral organ — healthy and bad eye as inner orientation of the whole person
Spiritual blindness and the closed eye — inner failure of perception and understanding
Eyes opened to spiritual reality — the inner moment of recognition and illumination
Desires of the eyes and envy — the eye as instrument of inner covetousness
Human jealousy and envy — the consuming inner force of resentment and possessive passion
Human zeal for God — inner passionate devotion as jealous advocacy
Divine jealousy and zeal — God's consuming inner claim on his people
Envying as inner disposition of competitive resentment
The envious gaze — inner resentment expressed as hostile watching
Zeal as passionate inner devotion — consuming fervour for God and his cause
Jealousy as inner hostility — envy, rivalry, and divisive passion
Divine jealousy — God's passionate inner claim on his people
Term names the state of being disfavoured or unloved — an inner relational orientation of lesser love or active disfavour that carries legal and relational consequence
Term names a settled inner hostile disposition — persistent hatred or grudge-bearing directed toward another person, driving external aggression or threatening intent
Term names hatred as an inner hostile disposition directed toward another person — a settled enmity that motivates action, can be concealed, and stands in direct contrast to love
Term names hatred as an attributed inner disposition of God toward a people — projected or perceived divine enmity used to interpret suffering or covenant relationship
Term names hatred as a fundamental inner human capacity — listed alongside love and envy as constitutive orientations of the person that define relational existence and cease at death
Term names hatred as an inner disposition — an established hostile attitude toward God or others that characterises the person's orientation and draws divine judgment
Term names the burning heat of indignation or intense suffering — whether the inner scorching of moral outrage at wickedness, or the burning of suffering as an inner experience
Term names indignation as an inner response to wrong — the rising of righteous anger produced by godly grief and repentance
Term names the provocation of human jealousy as a divine redemptive instrument — the inner state of jealousy stirred to draw the displaced toward returning to God
Term names the provocation of divine jealousy — the inner disposition of God stirred when his people turn to idols
Term describes inner state from troubled agitation to fierce rage — emotionally intense inner condition
Term names intense inner rage — emotionally charged anger at extreme moral intensity
Term names bearing divine indignation — accepting God's anger as just, waiting for vindication
Term names divine wrath as an inner state whose potential application motivates obedience
Term used of misplaced inner trust in material defences — false security rather than God
Term applied to inner self-mastery — the person without self-control is an undefended city
Term used of chaste self-possession bringing relational peace — inner character of purity
Term used of God's salvation as encompassing protection — inner security grounded in divine action
Term expresses God's continual attentiveness — walls of his people always before him
Term describes gift of prophetic resilience — the servant made a bronze wall to withstand opposition
Term names sullen vexation withdrawing from engagement — resentful displeasure manifesting in physical withdrawal
Term names inner disposition of zeal and earnestness — fervency of spirit actively pursuing godly goals, opposed to sloth
Term names earnestness as inner quality produced by godly grief — evidence of genuine love and faithfulness
Term names the inner haste of urgent desire — speed of inner urgency driving immediate action
Term names inner disposition of eager striving — pressing diligently toward a worthy goal out of inner conviction and commitment
Term names inner earnestness as a proven character quality — tested diligence heightened by confidence and trust in others
